message:
🌱 Add dragonfly: Healthcheck in DragonFly v1.22.0 is failing mission (#1285)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,77 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "dragonfly-3644-healthcheck-in-dragonfly-v1-22-0-is-failing",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "dragonfly: Healthcheck in DragonFly v1.22.0 is failing",
+    "description": "Healthcheck in DragonFly v1.22.0 is failing. Community-reported issue.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify dragonfly troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your dragonfly deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n dragonfly -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly -n dragonfly --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review dragonfly configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant dragonfly configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n dragonfly -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly\nkubectl get configmap -n dragonfly -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=dragonfly\n```\nThe `healthcheck.sh` in v1.22.0 is not returning healthy\n\n**To Reproduce**\nSteps to reproduce the behavior:\n1. Run v1.22.0 in EKS\n2."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Healthcheck in DragonFly v1.22.0 is failing",
+        "description": "as a workaround you can pass  the port you are using like this:\n`docker run -e HEALTHCHECK_PORT=6379 --read-only --group-add 999  --cap-drop=ALL --user 999  --rm docker.dragonflydb.io/dragonflydb/dragonfly:v1.22.2`\n```yaml\nActive Internet connections (only servers)\nProto Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address           Foreign Address         State       PID/Program name    \ntcp        0      0 0.0.0.0:6379            0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN      -\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Healthcheck in DragonFly v1.22.0 is failing is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=dragonfly -n dragonfly --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n dragonfly --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "as a workaround you can pass  the port you are using like this:\n`docker run -e HEALTHCHECK_PORT=6379 --read-only --group-add 999  --cap-drop=ALL --user 999  --rm docker.dragonflydb.io/dragonflydb/dragonfly:v1.22.2`",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "Active Internet connections (only servers)\nProto Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address           Foreign Address         State       PID/Program name    \ntcp        0      0 0.0.0.0:6379            0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN      -",
+        "Container dragonfly failed liveness probe, will be restarted\nReadiness probe failed: nc: missing port number",
+        "Warning  Unhealthy  3m28s                  kubelet            Readiness probe errored: rpc error: code = Unknown desc = failed to exec in container: container is in CONTAINER_EXITED state\nWarning  Unhealthy  2m49s (x8 over 3m49s)  kubelet            Readiness probe failed: nc: missing port number\nWarning  Unhealthy  2m49s (x6 over 3m49s)  kubelet            Liveness probe failed: nc: missing port number"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
